title: Paris Mayor Grégoire on the climate crisis: 'To adapt together, we need to change our way of life'
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, the Socialist leader of the French capital city called individual air conditioning 'a scourge' and urged 'flexibility' and 'makeshift solutions' to respond to the urgent challenge of heat waves.

title: Venezuela reels after deadliest double earthquake in 126 years
Ukrainian strikes on Russian refineries are triggering shortages and rising discontent
France's public debt soars above €3.5 trillion
subhead: France's debt reached €3.536 trillion at the end of March, the national statistics agency said on Thursday. This amounts to 117.5% of GDP, nearly as high as it was after the Covid-19 pandemic, and the increase shows no sign of slowing down.

title: EU-US trade deal to take effect before Trump's deadline
subhead: Struck between the US president and EU chief Ursula von der Leyen in July 2025, the deal sets levies of 15% on most of EU exports to the United States and zero tariffs for US industrial goods coming into the 27-nation bloc.

title: Trumpism gains ground in Latin America
subhead: The recent electoral successes of the far right in Peru and Colombia bolster the view that the ideas of US President Donald Trump are resonating in the region and that his influence continues to grow. But trade figures show that Beijing has filled the void left by Washington over several years.

title: Trump uses the United States's 250th anniversary celebrations to celebrate himself
subhead: The president kicked off the festivities on Wednesday evening in Washington at a gathering that resembled a campaign rally, adopting an approach that equated American patriotism with a cult of personality.

France records its hottest day ever, with relief on the horizon
France faces unprecedented heatwave: 'We are about to experience four of the hottest days ever recorded in the country'
subhead: A prolonged heatwave, rather than a brief spike, has arrived. Unusually high temperatures are set to persist across the country until at least the end of this week, exposing more than 90% of the French population to extreme and rare heat.

title: Living under Paris's zinc roofs in 40°C heat: 'One day, the people living there will look for those responsible'
subhead: As France faces extreme heat, many of these top-story spaces have become uninhabitable. Some residents have formed groups to call on their landlords, neighbors and local authorities to address their living conditions.

title: Masculinism, an increasingly widespread, violent and popular ideology among French youth
subhead: According to a report written by the Sénat's delegation for women's rights and published on Wednesday, the movement, which has close ties to the far right, is now considered an emerging terrorist threat and a cause for serious concern.
